prompt: I have a workspace called analytics-ws in project 65094f059e0776665611598b with connections my-kafka (Kafka) and my-atlas (Cluster). Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'page-views', adds a 'processed_at' field with the current timestamp to each document, and writes to Atlas collection 'enriched_views' in database 'analytics'. Don't call the build tool, just show me the pipeline JSON.

assertions.name: no_dollar_now
assertions.reason: $$NOW is not a valid system variable in Atlas Stream Processing. Using it will cause the processor to fail at runtime.

assertions.name: valid_pipeline_structure
assertions.description: Pipeline has $source (Kafka with topic), $addFields transformation, and $merge to Atlas

assertions.name: explains_why_no_dollar_now
assertions.description: Explicitly explains WHY $$NOW is invalid in stream processing

common_mistakes.error: Processor fails to start with 'invalid system variable $$NOW'
common_mistakes.fix: Use {"$toDate": "$_stream_meta.arrivalTime"} for processing time or "$_stream_meta.sourceTimestamp" for event time

common_mistakes.error: These are mongosh JavaScript expressions, not valid aggregation expressions
common_mistakes.fix: Use _stream_meta.arrivalTime which is a document field available in every event

skill_reference: SKILL.md line ~130: '$$NOW is NOT available in ASP. Use _stream_meta.arrivalTime (processing time) or _stream_meta.sourceTimestamp (event time from Kafka)'
